canonical_question: How does the middleware pipeline pattern work?
constraints: Middleware execution order is registration order -- reordering middleware changes behavior silently
Every middleware MUST call next() (or equivalent) or the request will hang or time out
Error-handling middleware must be registered separately from regular middleware in frameworks that distinguish them (Express uses 4-arg signature)
Never mutate shared state across concurrent requests -- use request-scoped context objects instead
Short-circuiting middleware (auth, rate-limit) must appear before the handlers they protect

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Looking for event-driven pub/sub or message broker patterns
Need to implement an API gateway with routing, load balancing, and service discovery

alternative_to.label: Decorator Pattern (OOP alternative for wrapping behavior)

often_confused_with.label: Message Queue / Event-Driven Architecture (async, not request/response)

inputs_needed.question: Which web framework or language are you using?
What cross-cutting concern do you need to add -- logging, auth, error handling, rate limiting, or something else?
